summary: The core create/publish path is implemented correctly and both required command gates passed, but the shipped API boundary is still looser than the documented contract. `POST /api/open/notes` validates types for `title`, `markdown`, and `isPublished`, yet it does not reject extra unsupported fields. The design doc says the body accepts only those fields and that non-conforming JSON is rejected before persistence. As shipped, callers can send out-of-scope fields such as `slug`, `tags`, or `summary` and still get `201`, with those fields silently ignored. That is a substantive contract mismatch for this boundary, so I would hold promotion.
satisfied_exit_criteria: A valid keyed request creates a private note when `isPublished` is omitted.
A valid keyed request publishes the note immediately when `isPublished` is `true`.
API-created notes enter the existing note AI enrichment lifecycle after save.
The response JSON includes the created note identity plus owner/public URLs as appropriate.
missing_requirements: Make the request-shape validation strict enough to reject non-conforming JSON with unsupported top-level fields, so the route truly accepts only `title`, `markdown`, and optional `isPublished` as documented.
Align the automated coverage with that boundary by adding a focused test that an otherwise valid keyed request containing out-of-scope fields is rejected rather than silently persisted.
human_review_triggers: The external note API currently accepts extra out-of-scope request fields instead of rejecting them, which weakens the boundary contract around remote-controlled slug/tags/summary inputs.
